snp4.10:1.2: is one said to be at peace?
snp4.10:1.4:
—when asked about— the ultimate person.”
The Buddha:
snp4.10:2.1: “Free from craving before the break-up [of the body],
snp4.10:3.4: he is a sage, his speech under control.
snp4.10:4.1: Free from attachment with regard to the future,
snp4.10:4.3: he sees seclusion in the midst of sensory contacts.
snp4.10:4.4: He can’t be led in terms of views.
snp4.10:5.4: he doesn’t engage in divisive speech.
snp4.10:7.1: Not in hopes of material gain does he take on the training;
snp4.10:7.2: when without material gain he isn’t upset.
snp4.10:7.4: he doesn’t through craving hunger for flavors.
snp4.10:8.4: No swellings of pride are his.
snp4.10:9.2: when, on knowing the Dhamma, he’s in- dependent;
snp4.10:9.3: in whom no craving is found
snp4.10:10.1: He is said to be at peace,
snp4.10:10.3: with nothing at all to tie him down:
snp4.10:11.3: In him you can’t pin down what’s embraced
snp4.10:11.4:
or rejected.
He has no preference
snp4.10:12.4: which is why he is unperturbed with regard to their words.
snp4.10:13.2: the sage doesn’t speak of himself as among those who are higher,
snp4.10:13.4: He, theory-free, goes to no theory.
snp4.10:14.1: For whom nothing in the world is his own,
snp4.10:14.2: who doesn’t grieve over what is not,
snp4.10:14.3: who doesn’t enter into doctrines phenomena:
snp4.10:14.4: He is said to be at peace.”
